{"key_event":"Intensified rubber terror and Force Publique expansion","reasoning":"The Congo Free State gained greater coercive and logistical reach through concessionary rule, militarization, and the Matadi–Léopoldville railway, though mutinies and international scrutiny kept capacity from becoming fully strong. Local society was further atomized by forced labor, hostage-taking, famine, flight, and coerced chiefs, with missionary and humanitarian networks only slightly offsetting this devastation.","society_change":-0.6,"society_power":1.4,"state_change":1.0,"state_power":5.0}
